---
title: "marquee_labels_from_colors"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L1667)

# `marquee_labels_from_colors()`

Create Marquee-Formatted Labels from a Color Vector

## Usage

```r
marquee_labels_from_colors(x, colors, bold = FALSE, italic = FALSE)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `x` | Character vector of values to format (must match names in `colors`). |
| `colors` | Named vector of colors (names = category levels). |
| `bold` | Logical, make text bold. Default: FALSE. |
| `italic` | Logical, make text italic. Default: FALSE. |

## Value

Character vector of marquee-formatted labels.

## Description

Takes a named color vector and a vector of values, returning
marquee-formatted labels where each value's text color matches
its corresponding fill color. Useful for labeling bars with
color-matched text.

## Examples

```r
fill_colors <- c(
    "Apples" = "#E63946",
    "Oranges" = "#F4A261",
    "Bananas" = "#E9C46A"
)

marquee_labels_from_colors(c("Apples", "Oranges"), fill_colors, bold = TRUE)
# Returns: c("{#E63946 **Apples**}", "{#F4A261 **Oranges**}")
```
